summary: The NSG “ogedemo-security-nsg” currently permits inbound SSH (port 22) and RDP (port 3389) from any IP address, exposing management ports to the internet. We recommend restricting these rules to a known management IP CIDR block.
root_cause: Inbound security rules “Allow-SSH” and “Allow-RDP” have sourceAddressPrefix set to ‘*’, allowing 0.0.0.0/0 access to ports 22 and 3389.
risk: If the managementIpCidr parameter is misconfigured or too narrow, administrators may lock themselves out of SSH/RDP; static IP changes require updating this parameter.
verification: Run `az network nsg rule list -g OGEDemos_RG --nsg-name ogedemo-security-nsg` and confirm that the sourceAddressPrefix for rules Allow-SSH and Allow-RDP matches the approved CIDR. Attempt SSH/RDP from an allowed IP and verify denied from an external IP.
human_review_focus: Validate the managementIpCidr parameter is correct
Ensure rule names and priorities match existing policies
Check for overlapping or higher-priority NSG rules that could re-open ports
